title: centaurea pratensis subsp. decipiens
intro: Centaurea pratensis subsp. decipiens, also known as knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the aster family, characterized by its purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea pratensis subsp. decipiens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
